title: Titles, Canonicals, Robots Directives, Hreflang, and Structured Metadata · Mastery Check
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss TypeScript decisions for titles, canonicals, robots directives, hreflang, and structured metadata and accepts only compiler plus runtime changed-case evidence, explicit non-claims, and named network, DNS, browser, accessibility, security, load, legal-review, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for evidence-linked indexing and metadata audit. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, cha…
objectives: Audit title and description presence, uniqueness, length evidence, visible consistency, and stakeholder intent without ranking promises.
Resolve canonical links and reconcile redirects, sitemap URLs, internal links, content identity, and scope before reporting conflicts.
Parse robots meta and X-Robots-Tag directives by agent, precedence, response, and scope while keeping crawl and index policy distinct.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
